data.title: Bitcoin's Security Evolution Against Quantum Attacks
data.description:
The article delves into the potential threat of quantum attacks on Bitcoin and explores strategies for enhancing its security through algorithm upgrades. According to PANews, the focus is on understanding the time window during which quantum attacks could pose a significant risk and identifying pathways to counter these threats effectively.

The discussion highlights the importance of upgrading Bitcoin's cryptographic algorithms to withstand quantum computing capabilities. This evolution is crucial to ensure the continued security and integrity of Bitcoin transactions and holdings.

Experts emphasize the need for proactive measures to safeguard Bitcoin against future quantum threats, suggesting that timely upgrades and innovations in cryptographic techniques are essential for maintaining the cryptocurrency's resilience.

data.title: Japan's Financial Services Agency (FSA) has released new cybersecurity regulations for cryptocurrency exchanges, strengthening their three-tiered protection framework.
data.description: Japan's Financial Services Agency (FSA) released a policy on strengthening cybersecurity for companies such as cryptocurrency exchanges. With the core objective of protecting investor assets, the policy proposes a three-tiered security protection system: self-help, collaborative assistance, and public assistance. This system corresponds to collaborative protection by the companies themselves, self-regulatory organizations, and regulatory agencies, respectively. The policy points out that in addition to the leakage of signature keys such as mnemonic phrases, the risks of sophisticated attack methods such as social engineering attacks and intrusions into outsourced service providers are increasing, urging improvements in overall defense capabilities. Simultaneously, Japan plans to incorporate crypto-related scenarios into the "Delta Wall" cross-industry cybersecurity exercise to enhance the industry's emergency response capabilities. (CoinPost)

data.title: Crypto Market Experiences Significant Liquidations Over 24 Hours
data.description:
In the past 24 hours, the cryptocurrency market has seen substantial liquidations amounting to $72.1 million, according to ChainCatcher. Data from Coinglass reveals that long positions accounted for $36.95 million of the liquidations, while short positions totaled $35.15 million.

Bitcoin positions were notably affected, with long liquidations reaching $8.39 million and short liquidations at $8.04 million. Ethereum also experienced significant liquidations, with $6.83 million in long positions and $5.45 million in short positions being liquidated.

data.title: Coinglass Q1 Crypto Market Report: Total Trading Volume Reaches $20.57 Trillion, Binance Leads in Multiple Dimensions
data.description: A recent report from Coinglass shows that the total trading volume of the crypto market in the first quarter of 2026 was approximately $20.57 trillion, of which spot trading accounted for approximately $1.94 trillion and derivatives for approximately $18.63 trillion. The ratio of derivatives to spot trading volume was approximately 9.6 times, indicating that market activity remained primarily concentrated in the derivatives sector. The report points out that Binance maintained its leading position in several key metrics. Its first-quarter derivatives trading volume was approximately $4.90 trillion, accounting for approximately 34.9% of the top ten exchanges; its average open interest was approximately $23.9 billion, accounting for approximately 29.9%; and its user assets totaled approximately $152.9 billion, accounting for approximately 73.5% of major exchanges. Overall, the market has formed a structure with Binance at the top, OKX as the core of the second tier, and other platforms distributed in the secondary tier.

data.title: Japan's Financial Authority Unveils Cybersecurity Strategy for Crypto Platforms
data.description:
Japan's Financial Services Agency has announced a new cybersecurity strategy for cryptocurrency trading platforms. According to Foresight News, this initiative is based on 18 public opinions collected between February and March 2026, with the primary goal of protecting investors' assets. The strategy introduces a three-tier security enhancement framework: self-help (individual operators), mutual assistance (self-regulatory organizations), and public assistance (regulatory authorities).

The agency highlighted that cyberattacks have evolved from simple key theft to more sophisticated methods such as social engineering and supply chain intrusions, making traditional cold wallet management insufficient for security. Moving forward, the Financial Services Agency plans to conduct threat-led penetration testing (TLPT) on major operators and intends to revise operational guidelines to improve cybersecurity staffing and external audit standards.

data.title: The Drift team's associated address transferred 56.25 million DRIFT tokens, worth approximately $2.44 million, to an exchange.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, since the Drift Protocol security incident, wallet addresses associated with its team have transferred 56.25 million DRIFT tokens, worth approximately $2.44 million, to Bybit and Gate.

data.title: U.S. Stocks Close Mixed as TRON Surges Over 11%
data.description: U.S. stocks ended the trading session with mixed results, reflecting varied performances across different sectors. According to NS3.AI, the Dow Jones Industrial Average experienced a slight decline of 0.13%, while the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite saw modest gains of 0.11% and 0.18%, respectively. In the crypto-related stock sector, TRON stood out with a significant increase of over 11.37%, highlighting a divergence in performance within the sector.

data.title: AI-Driven Stablecoin Savings Vault Launched on Polygon
data.description: Polygon has announced the launch of SurfLiquid, an AI-driven stablecoin savings vault on its blockchain. According to Foresight News, this new product allows users to have individual non-custodial accounts, enabling them to earn returns by holding USDC in the lending market. The vault is now operational on Polygon, with deployment support provided by Morpho.

data.title: Qatar Declines Role in U.S.-Iran Ceasefire Negotiations
data.description:
Qatar has declined to act as a key mediator in potential ceasefire talks between the United States and Iran, complicating efforts to find a diplomatic resolution. According to BlockBeats, officials and mediators familiar with the situation revealed that Qatar informed U.S. officials last week of its decision not to play a leading role in the mediation process.

Mediators stated on Friday that the latest round of efforts, led by Pakistan, to facilitate a ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran has reached an impasse. Iran has formally informed mediators that it is unwilling to meet with U.S. officials in Islamabad in the coming days, deeming the American demands unacceptable.

Turkey and Egypt continue to seek solutions and are considering new locations for talks, such as Doha or Istanbul, along with new proposals to break the deadlock. Additionally, according to Iranian media Fars News, sources indicate that Iran has rejected a U.S. proposal for a 48-hour ceasefire.

data.title: Vietnam's Q1 GDP Growth Exceeds Expectations
data.description: Vietnam's GDP grew by 7.83% year-on-year in the first quarter, surpassing the expected 7.50%, according to Jin10. This growth rate, however, is lower than the previous quarter's 8.46%. The data reflects Vietnam's continued economic expansion, although at a slightly reduced pace compared to the last quarter.

data.title: Vietnam's March Exports Surge by 20% Year-on-Year
data.description: Vietnam's export growth in March reached 20% year-on-year, surpassing expectations of 14.40%, according to Jin10. This marks a significant increase from the previous month's growth rate of 5.70%. The robust performance highlights Vietnam's strengthening position in the global trade market.

data.title: FTX/Alameda Sells Drift Tokens Following $285 Million Theft
data.description: Following a $285 million theft incident at Drift Protocol, FTX/Alameda sold 6.94 million DRIFT tokens, valued at $320,000, through Wintermute six hours ago. Blockchain sleuth EmberCN posted on X. Alameda, an investor in Drift Protocol, had acquired 8.33 million DRIFT tokens a year ago through vesting unlocks, which were valued at $6.22 million at the time. However, the value of these tokens has since decreased to $380,000.

data.title: AI Infrastructure Shift Highlights Storage as Key Asset
data.description:
In the backdrop of the ongoing surge in AI computing power, market attention is subtly shifting from GPUs to a more fundamental bottleneck—storage. According to PANews, the launch of the DRAM ETF marks not just the introduction of a new product but also the first time the capital market has priced 'storage' as an independent asset class.

This article delves into the true significance behind this ETF from three perspectives: industrial logic, capital structure, and market sentiment. It represents a direct reflection of the evolution of AI infrastructure and serves as a crucial entry point for global funds to reallocate towards leading storage companies. Additionally, it may become an important indicator of whether the market has entered a crowded trading phase.

As storage transitions from a 'supporting role' to a 'scarce layer,' the reevaluation of AI core assets may just be beginning.

data.title: US stocks closed mixed in the crypto sector, with TRON rising over 11.37%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, at the close of trading on the US stock market, the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.13%, the S&P 500 rose 0.11%, and the Nasdaq Composite rose 0.18%. The crypto sector was mixed, with SBET falling 4.18%, MSTR falling 2.4%, TRON rising over 11.37%, and MARA rising over 8.33%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: Middle East Tensions Impact Oil and Gold Markets, CICC Reports
data.description:
CICC's recent report highlights the impact of the U.S.-Iran conflict on oil prices, noting that the surge in oil prices has heightened inflation risks. According to Jin10, market expectations regarding the Federal Reserve's interest rate cut path have shifted, leading to selling pressure on gold ETFs, which had seen significant increases last year. Additionally, liquidity shocks in the futures and options markets have contributed to short-term corrections.

The current geopolitical situation in the Middle East is at a critical juncture, with oil prices facing potential fluctuations. The gold market's pricing focus may shift towards assessing the impact of supply shocks on economic stagnation, with previously priced-in interest rate hike expectations potentially needing adjustment.

Looking ahead, CICC suggests that whether the geopolitical situation de-escalates leading to an oil price correction, monetary policy returns to a more accommodative stance, or supply shocks exacerbate recession pressures, highlighting gold's safe-haven value, there may be room for upward adjustments in gold investment demand and prices.

data.title: Circle Mints Additional USDC on Solana Network
data.description: Circle has minted an additional 250 million USDC on the Solana network. According to ChainCatcher, this brings the total amount minted over the past month to 10.25 billion USDC.

data.title: FTX sold 6.94 million DRIFTs through Windemute, worth approximately $320,000.
data.description: According to crypto analyst EmberCN, following the approximately $285 million security incident at Drift Protocol, FTX/Alameda sold 6.94 million DRIFT tokens via Wintermute six hours ago, worth about $320,000. Data shows that Alameda, as an investor, previously acquired approximately 8.33 million DRIFT tokens through vesting unlocks, initially worth about $6.22 million; their current value has decreased to approximately $380,000.

data.title: The address associated with Erik Voorhees purchased another 431.8 ETH, bringing his total holdings to over 122,000 ETH.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, a wallet address associated with Erik Voorhees spent approximately $887,900 USDT to purchase 431.8 ETH five hours ago. Previously, this address had accumulated purchases of 122,355 ETH, worth approximately $264 million.

data.title: Erik Voorhees-Linked Wallet Makes Significant Ethereum Purchase
data.description: A wallet associated with Erik Voorhees has made another substantial Ethereum purchase. According to Odaily, the wallet spent approximately $887,900 USDT to acquire 431.8 ETH five hours ago. Previously, this wallet had accumulated a total of 122,355 ETH.
